comment_text.sn8.7:1.1: Normally I use the gender-neutral and literal “mendicant” for bhikkhu, but at this formal act of the Saṅgha only men would be present, so I use “monk”. Nuns hold their own ceremony.
comment_text.sn8.7:1.2: This was the end of the rains retreat, during which mendicants stayed in one place. To conclude the retreat, the mendicants gather to invite admonition from each other, clearing the air of any conflicts or tensions that might have arisen during the retreat. This is done instead of the regular recitation of the Pātimokkha rules. Discussed in detail at Kd 4.
comment_text.sn8.7:1.5: The Buddha does not hold himself above accountability. Rather, a good spiritual leader shows by example that everyone must be open to criticism.

root_text.sn8.7:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ pañcamattehi bhikkhusatehi sabbeheva arahantehi.
root_text.sn8.7:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā tadahuposathe pannarase pavāraṇāya bhikkhusaṅghaparivuto abbhokāse nisinno hoti.
root_text.sn8.7:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā tuṇhībhūtaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ anuviloketvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn8.7:1.5: Na ca me kiñci garahatha kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn8.7:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.7:2.2: “na kho mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavato kiñci garahāma kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā.
root_text.sn8.7:2.3: Bhagavā hi, bhante, anuppannassa maggassa uppādetā, asañjātassa maggassa sañjanetā, anakkhātassa maggassa akkhātā, maggaññū maggavidū maggakovido.
root_text.sn8.7:2.4: Maggānugā ca, bhante, etarahi sāvakā viharanti pacchā samannāgatā;
root_text.sn8.7:2.6: Na ca me bhagavā kiñci garahati kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn8.7:3.1: “Na khvāhaṁ te, sāriputta, kiñci garahāmi kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā.
root_text.sn8.7:3.2: Paṇḍito tvaṁ, sāriputta, mahāpañño tvaṁ, sāriputta, puthupañño tvaṁ, sāriputta, hāsapañño tvaṁ, sāriputta, javanapañño tvaṁ, sāriputta, tikkhapañño tvaṁ, sāriputta, nibbedhikapañño tvaṁ, sāriputta.
root_text.sn8.7:3.3: Seyyathāpi, sāriputta, rañño cakkavattissa jeṭṭhaputto pitarā pavattitaṁ cakkaṁ sammadeva anuppavatteti;
root_text.sn8.7:3.4: evameva kho tvaṁ, sāriputta, mayā anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ sammadeva anuppavattesī”ti.
root_text.sn8.7:4.1: “No ce kira me, bhante, bhagavā kiñci garahati kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā.
root_text.sn8.7:4.2: Imesaṁ pana, bhante, bhagavā pañcannaṁ bhikkhusatānaṁ na kiñci garahati kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn8.7:4.3: “Imesampi khvāhaṁ, sāriputta, pañcannaṁ bhikkhusatānaṁ na kiñci garahāmi kāyikaṁ vā vācasikaṁ vā.
root_text.sn8.7:4.4: Imesañhi, sāriputta, pañcannaṁ bhikkhusatānaṁ saṭṭhi bhikkhū tevijjā, saṭṭhi bhikkhū chaḷabhiññā, saṭṭhi bhikkhū ubhatobhāgavimuttā, atha itare paññāvimuttā”ti.
root_text.sn8.7:5.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.7:5.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso bhagavantaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.sn8.7:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery, together with a large Saṅgha of around five hundred monks, all of whom were perfected ones.
translation_text.sn8.7:1.2: Now, at that time it was the sabbath—the full moon on the fifteenth day—and the Buddha was sitting in the open surrounded by the Saṅgha of monks for the invitation to admonish.
translation_text.sn8.7:1.3: Then the Buddha looked around the Saṅgha of monks, who were silent. He addressed them:
translation_text.sn8.7:1.4: “Come now, monks, I invite you all:
translation_text.sn8.7:1.5: Is there anything I’ve done by way of body or speech that you would criticize?”
translation_text.sn8.7:2.1: When he had spoken, Venerable Sāriputta got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said:
translation_text.sn8.7:2.2: “There is nothing, sir, that you’ve done by way of body or speech that we would criticize.
translation_text.sn8.7:2.3: For the Blessed One gave rise to the unarisen path, gave birth to the unborn path, and explained the unexplained path. He is the knower of the path, the discoverer of the path, the expert on the path.
translation_text.sn8.7:2.4: And now the disciples live following the path; they acquire it later.
translation_text.sn8.7:2.5: And sir, I invite the Blessed One.
translation_text.sn8.7:2.6: Is there anything I’ve done by way of body or speech that you would criticize?”
translation_text.sn8.7:3.1: “There is nothing, Sāriputta, that you’ve done by way of body or speech that I would criticize.
translation_text.sn8.7:3.2: Sāriputta, you are astute. You have great wisdom, widespread wisdom, laughing wisdom, swift wisdom, sharp wisdom, penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.sn8.7:3.3: A wheel-turning monarch’s oldest son rightly keeps wielding the power set in motion by his father.
translation_text.sn8.7:3.4: In the same way, Sāriputta rightly keeps rolling the supreme Wheel of Dhamma that was rolled forth by me.”
translation_text.sn8.7:4.1: “Since it seems I have done nothing worthy of the Blessed One’s criticism,
translation_text.sn8.7:4.2: is there anything these five hundred monks have done by way of body or speech that you would criticize?”
translation_text.sn8.7:4.3: “There is nothing, Sāriputta, that these five hundred monks have done by way of body or speech that I would criticize.
translation_text.sn8.7:4.4: For of these five hundred monks, sixty have the three knowledges, sixty have the six direct knowledges, sixty are freed both ways, and the rest are freed by wisdom.”
translation_text.sn8.7:5.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn8.7:5.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn8.7:5.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.sn8.7:5.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled the Buddha in his presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.sn8.7:6.2: five hundred monks have gathered together <j>to purify their precepts.
translation_text.sn8.7:6.3: These untroubled sages <j>have cut off their fetters and bonds,
translation_text.sn8.7:6.4: they will not be reborn again.
translation_text.sn8.7:8.1: So disciples with the three knowledges,
translation_text.sn8.7:8.3: revere the winner of the battle,
translation_text.sn8.7:9.1: All are sons of the Blessed One—
translation_text.sn8.7:9.3: I bow to the Kinsman of the Sun,
translation_text.sn8.7:9.4: destroyer of the dart of craving.”
